questions.text: Where does the heart lie within the thoracic cavity?

answers.text: In the pleural cavity, between the lungs
In the mediastinum, between the lungs
In the pericardial cavity, posterior to the sternum
In the abdominal cavity, above the diaphragm

questions.text: Approximately what fraction of the heart lies to the left of the sternum?
Which structure forms the apex of the heart?

answers.text: The tip of the left ventricle

questions.text: What is the primary function of the fibrous pericardium?

answers.text: To produce serous fluid for lubrication
To anchor the heart and prevent overstretching
To supply blood to the myocardium
To generate the electrical impulse for contraction

questions.text: The pericardial cavity contains approximately how much serous fluid?
Which layer of the heart wall is the thickest and responsible for the pumping action?
Which chamber of the heart has the thickest walls?
What is the function of the chordae tendineae?

answers.text: To generate the cardiac action potential
To connect the AV valves to papillary muscles
To separate the atria from the ventricles
To supply blood to the myocardium

questions.text: What does the coronary sulcus separate?

answers.text: The right ventricle from the left ventricle
The right atrium from the left atrium
The aorta from the pulmonary artery

questions.text: What is the function of the fibrous skeleton of the heart?

answers.text: To prevent premature conduction from atria to ventricles
To store calcium for cardiac contraction
To pump blood through the coronary arteries
